caveat: This framework content pack is a structured starter representation and does not constitute legal advice or complete regulatory coverage. This is a starter content pack for scoping purposes only. It does not constitute legal advice or a complete compliance determination.

obligations.title: Establish AI governance roles
obligations.description: Define accountability and governance roles for AI risk management activities.
obligations.applicability_notes: Applies to organizations designing, deploying, or operating AI systems.

obligations.title: Adopt AI risk management policy
obligations.description: Document policy commitments for trustworthy AI outcomes, oversight, and escalation.
obligations.applicability_notes: Should align with enterprise governance and legal requirements.

obligations.title: Define AI lifecycle controls
obligations.description: Define required controls and checkpoints across design, development, deployment, and retirement.
obligations.applicability_notes: Include controls for data, model, and system operations.

obligations.title: Map intended use and context
obligations.description: Document intended purpose, users, environment, and constraints for each AI use case.
obligations.applicability_notes: Include operational boundaries and fallback behavior.

obligations.title: Identify affected stakeholders
obligations.description: Identify stakeholders potentially impacted by system performance and outcomes.
obligations.applicability_notes: Consider downstream and indirect impact populations.

obligations.title: Catalog AI system risks
obligations.description: Map technical, operational, legal, and societal risks associated with AI use.
obligations.applicability_notes: Should include misuse and adversarial concerns.

obligations.title: Define measurement criteria
obligations.description: Define quantitative and qualitative metrics for reliability, safety, validity, and fairness.
obligations.applicability_notes: Metrics should be reproducible and tracked over time.

obligations.title: Evaluate data and model quality
obligations.description: Assess data quality, representativeness, and model performance limitations for intended use.

obligations.title: Monitor for drift and degradation
obligations.description: Continuously monitor operational behavior, drift, and unintended outcomes.

obligations.title: Implement risk treatment plans
obligations.description: Define and execute risk treatment plans with owners, due dates, and verification criteria.

obligations.title: Establish incident response for AI harms
obligations.description: Create incident response playbooks for AI-related failures, harms, or policy violations.
obligations.applicability_notes: Include rollback, containment, and communication paths.

obligations.title: Apply change management to AI systems
obligations.description: Require review and approval for material model, data, and prompt/configuration changes.

obligations.title: Maintain AI documentation and traceability
obligations.description: Maintain documentation linking requirements, data, models, evaluations, and approvals.
obligations.applicability_notes: Supports internal audits and external assessments.

obligations.title: Evaluate human oversight effectiveness
obligations.description: Assess adequacy of human oversight and intervention mechanisms in high-impact decisions.
obligations.applicability_notes: Define override and escalation authority boundaries.

obligations.title: Report AI risk posture to governance forums
obligations.description: Provide periodic reporting on AI risk posture, control effectiveness, and unresolved issues.
obligations.applicability_notes: Align reporting cadence with enterprise risk governance.

content_versions.obligation_text: Establish accountable AI governance roles and responsibilities.
Document AI risk governance policy and oversight expectations.
Implement lifecycle controls across AI design and operations.
Document intended use, context, and system boundaries.
Identify affected stakeholders and impact pathways.
Map and catalog AI risk scenarios.
Define measurement criteria for trustworthy AI characteristics.
Evaluate data/model quality and limitations for intended use.
Monitor operational drift and degradation continuously.
Create and track AI risk treatment plans.
Maintain AI incident response playbooks and escalation controls.
Apply change management to material AI modifications.
Maintain AI documentation and traceability artifacts.
Assess effectiveness of human oversight mechanisms.
Report AI risk posture to governance stakeholders.
